multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/sen.name: sentinel
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/sen.description: A numeric handle of a system object which will become “ready” when the process ends. You can use this value if you want to wait on several events at once using multiprocessing.connection.wait(). Otherwise calling join() is simpler. On Windows, this is an OS handle usable with the WaitForSingleObject and WaitForMultipleObjects family of API calls. On POSIX, this is a file descriptor usable with primitives from the select module. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: A numeric handle of a system object which will become “ready” when the process ends. You can use this value if you want to wait on several events at once using multiprocessing.connection.wait(). Other...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Semaphore.name: Semaphore
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Semaphore.description: A semaphore object: a close analog of threading.Semaphore. Instantiating this class may set the global start method. See Global start method for more details. A solitary difference from its close analog exists: its acquire method’s first argument is named block, as is consistent with Lock.acquire(). get_value()¶ Return the current value of semaphore. Note that this may raise NotImplementedError on platforms like macOS where sem_getvalue() is not implemented. locked()¶ Return a boolean indicat...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SimpleQueue.name: SimpleQueue
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SimpleQueue.description: It is a simplified Queue type, very close to a locked Pipe. Instantiating this class may set the global start method. See Global start method for more details. close()¶ Close the queue: release internal resources. A queue must not be used anymore after it is closed. For example, get(), put() and empty() methods must no longer be called. Added in version 3.9. empty()¶ Return True if the queue is empty, False otherwise. Always raises an OSError if the SimpleQueue is closed. get()¶ Remove and re...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.name: SyncManager
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.description: A subclass of BaseManager which can be used for the synchronization of processes. Objects of this type are returned by multiprocessing.Manager(). Its methods create and return Proxy Objects for a number of commonly used data types to be synchronized across processes. This notably includes shared lists and dictionaries. Barrier(parties[, action[, timeout]])¶ Create a shared threading.Barrier object and return a proxy for it. Added in version 3.3. BoundedSemaphore([value])¶ Create a shared thre...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_executa.name: set_executable
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_executa.description: Set the path of the Python interpreter to use when starting a child process. (By default sys.executable is used). Embedders will probably need to do something like set_executable(os.path.join(sys.exec_prefix, 'pythonw.exe')) before they can create child processes. Changed in version 3.4: Now supported on POSIX when the 'spawn' start method is used. Changed in version 3.11: Accepts a path-like object.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_forkser.name: set_forkserver_preload
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_forkser.description: Set a list of module names for the forkserver main process to attempt to import so that their already imported state is inherited by forked processes. Any ImportError when doing so is silently ignored. This can be used as a performance enhancement to avoid repeated work in every process. For this to work, it must be called before the forkserver process has been launched (before creating a Pool or starting a Process). Only meaningful when using the 'forkserver' start method. See Contexts and s...

info.excerpt: Set a list of module names for the forkserver main process to attempt to import so that their already imported state is inherited by forked processes. Any ImportError when doing so is silently ignored...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_start_m.name: set_start_method
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/set_start_m.description: Set the method which should be used to start child processes. The method argument can be 'fork', 'spawn' or 'forkserver'. Raises RuntimeError if the start method has already been set and force is not True. If method is None and force is True then the start method is set to None. If method is None and force is False then the context is set to the default context. Note that this should be called at most once, and it should be protected inside the if __name__ == '__main__' clause of the main mod...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/synchronize.name: synchronized
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/synchronize.description: Return a process-safe wrapper object for a ctypes object which uses lock to synchronize access. If lock is None (the default) then a multiprocessing.RLock object is created automatically. ctx is a context object, or None (use the current context). If None, calling this may set the global start method. See Global start method for more details. A synchronized wrapper will have two methods in addition to those of the object it wraps: get_obj() returns the wrapped object and get_lock() returns th...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.name: Semaphore
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.description: Create a shared threading.Semaphore object and return a proxy for it.

info.excerpt: Create a shared threading.Semaphore object and return a proxy for it.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Connection/.name: send
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Connection/.description: Send an object to the other end of the connection which should be read using recv(). The object must be picklable. Very large pickles (approximately 32 MiB+, though it depends on the OS) may raise a ValueError exception.

info.excerpt: Send an object to the other end of the connection which should be read using recv(). The object must be picklable. Very large pickles (approximately 32 MiB+, though it depends on the OS) may raise a V...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Connection/.name: send_bytes
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Connection/.description: Send byte data from a bytes-like object as a complete message. If offset is given then data is read from that position in buffer. If size is given then that many bytes will be read from buffer. Very large buffers (approximately 32 MiB+, though it depends on the OS) may raise a ValueError exception

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.name: set
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.description: Create a shared set object and return a proxy for it. Added in version 3.14: set support was added.

info.excerpt: Create a shared set object and return a proxy for it. Added in version 3.14: set support was added.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.name: shutdown
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.description: Stop the process used by the manager. This is only available if start() has been used to start the server process. This can be called multiple times.

info.excerpt: Stop the process used by the manager. This is only available if start() has been used to start the server process. This can be called multiple times.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/starma.name: starmap
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/starma.description: Like map() except that the elements of the iterable are expected to be iterables that are unpacked as arguments. Hence an iterable of [(1,2), (3, 4)] results in [func(1,2), func(3,4)]. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Like map() except that the elements of the iterable are expected to be iterables that are unpacked as arguments. Hence an iterable of [(1,2), (3, 4)] results in [func(1,2), func(3,4)]. Added in versio...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/starma.name: starmap_async
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/starma.description: A combination of starmap() and map_async() that iterates over iterable of iterables and calls func with the iterables unpacked. Returns a result object. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: A combination of starmap() and map_async() that iterates over iterable of iterables and calls func with the iterables unpacked. Returns a result object. Added in version 3.3.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/sta.name: start
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/sta.description: Start the process’s activity. This must be called at most once per process object. It arranges for the object’s run() method to be invoked in a separate process.

info.excerpt: Start the process’s activity. This must be called at most once per process object. It arranges for the object’s run() method to be invoked in a separate process.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.name: start
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.description: Start a subprocess to start the manager. If initializer is not None then the subprocess will call initializer(*initargs) when it starts.

info.excerpt: Start a subprocess to start the manager. If initializer is not None then the subprocess will call initializer(*initargs) when it starts.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/AsyncResult.name: successful
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/AsyncResult.description: Return whether the call completed without raising an exception. Will raise ValueError if the result is not ready. Changed in version 3.7: If the result is not ready, ValueError is raised instead of AssertionError.
